per_theorem_calls.label: Lemma 1: factual map and common-law pasting

published_prior_art.cite: tan_blanchet_syrgkanis_2026_prte_ot — generalized-Roy CCOT supplies the closest global-feasibility formulation, but not this finite rank-restricted array-pasting lemma.
tan_blanchet_syrgkanis_2026_prte_ot — the extreme-threshold marginal identity is a routine normalization consequence.

per_theorem_calls.label: Open-ended Question 1: sharp association sign frontier

published_prior_art.cite: tan_blanchet_syrgkanis_2026_prte_ot — sharp generalized-Roy PRTE bounds reduce product costs, not the proposed nonseparable gain-band association-loss frontier.
tan_blanchet_syrgkanis_2026_prte_ot — separable/product-cost reductions are the anchor's established regime.

per_theorem_calls.label: Open-ended Question 2: orthogonal inference for sign radius

published_prior_art.cite: ober_reynolds_2023_joint_po_ot — establishes root-n dual-OT inference for joint-potential-outcome bounds, but not generated generalized-Roy cutpoints or this sign-radius functional.

novelty_flags.label: Open-ended Questions 1–2 / related_work
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses Lin, Gao, Blanchet, and Glynn (AISTATS 2026), which gives causal conditional-OT identification and estimation with covariates, and Fan, Pass, and Shi (2025), which gives an OT identified-set characterization for incomplete-data moment models; state their precise non-overlap with the generalized-Roy association frontier.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 2: orthogonal inference for sign radius
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Inference is conditional on the unresolved common-law sharpness/sign-frontier result in Open-ended Question 1 and on the stated finite-grid LP regularity regime.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Root-n and directional inference are claimed only conditional on the sharp finite-grid generalized-Roy association-frontier characterization.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Lin–Gao–Blanchet–Glynn (2026) and Fan–Pass–Shi (2025), then specify why their conditional/general OT results neither impose the generalized-Roy factual map nor deliver the association-loss sign radius.

State a concrete empirical design example satisfying saturated cutpoints, including the required p=0 and p=1 arms, and add an unsaturated-support extension or an explicit statement that it becomes an outer bound.
Sharpen the inference question by naming sufficient finite-cell rates for the outcome-cell and threshold estimators and by stating the exact influence-function target for the reduced LP value.

recommended_next_step: Revise the published-literature positioning around recent causal conditional-OT work, retain the conditional inference scope, then advance the field-tier kernel to Stage 0.
